title: Aglaia meridionalis
intro: Aglaia meridionalis is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the Merida aglaia or Philippine aglaia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Aglaia meridionalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, including aglavin, aglacones, and meridionalin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture or dried extract, although its use should be carefully considered under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
taxonomy: Aglaia meridionalis is classified within the family Meliaceae, which includes other members such as mahogany and neem trees. This family is part of the larger order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the soapberry family. Within the family Meliaceae, Aglaia meridionalis is placed in the genus Aglaia, which comprises around 120 species of flowering trees. The genus Aglaia is further divided into several sections, with Aglaia meridionalis being a part of one of these sections. Further classification of Aglaia meridionalis may include its species rank and any additional taxonomic details.
uses: Aglaia meridionalis, a plant native to the tropical regions, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its extracts have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The plant's bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and triterpenoids, are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and its safety for human consumption. Additionally, Aglaia meridionalis is being investigated for its anti-cancer properties due to its ability to inhibit cell proliferation.
properties: Aglaia meridionalis is known for its potential therapeutic properties, which may include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. constituents: Aglaia meridionalis contains a variety of active constituents, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds. These bioactive molecules are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Research has identified specific compounds such as aglaiol and aglaiol acetate in Aglaia meridionalis, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The exact composition and concentration of these active constituents can vary depending on factors like the plant's geographic location and growing conditions. Further studies are needed to fully understand the pharmacological effects and potential applications of Aglaia meridionalis.

side_effects: Aglaia meridionalis is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, but its use may be associated with potential side effects due to its toxic compounds. These compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, can cause gastrointestinal problems, allergic reactions, and other adverse effects. Ingestion of large amounts of the plant can lead to more severe side effects, such as cardiovascular problems and liver damage. Furthermore, Aglaia meridionalis may interact with other medications, exacerbating side effects or reducing its efficacy. More research is necessary to fully understand the side effects of Aglaia meridionalis and to ensure safe use of the plant in medicinal applications.
